title: VJ Edgecombe, 76ers get hot, steal Game 2 against ice-cold Celtics: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Boston Celtics Jaylen Brown mentioned Edgecombe by name three times unprompted as he explained what went wrong for the Celtics.

Philadelphia 76ers guard Vj Edgecombe takes a shot against Boston Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman (55) and forward Sam Hauser (30) during the first half of Game 2 of a first-round NBA playoffs series, Tuesday, April 21, 2026, in Boston.

title: 24 hours after the 2026 Boston Marathon, the barefoot runner’s feet are feeling ‘great’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News “The only thing that hurts is, honestly, like my quads, my feet feel great though,” Concannon said.
Terrence Concannon running the 2026 Boston Marathon barefoot.
Terrence Concannon A man who ran the 2026 Boston Marathon barefoot is now recovering, and his feet are somehow feeling “great,” the runner said.

title: A Back Bay church says its ‘God loves trans people’ banner was stolen 3 times in a few weeks
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News “This is something that we feel like it's important to be loud about, because certainly, the forces of hate are being pretty loud right now.
" Old South Church ShareSend this article to your social connections.
A “God loves trans people” sign displayed outside a Back Bay church has been stolen three times in recent weeks, the church said, but church leadership said they won’t stop sharing the message.

title: Morgan Geekie scores bizarre Bruins goal after Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen lets in puck from center ice

Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen had a night to forget on Tuesday.

title: ‘This is our f–ing city’: Backlash ensues after DHS uses Fenway Park photo in social media post
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News “This is our f–ing city, and nobody is going to dictate our freedom,” Rep.
Seth Moulton wrote.
(Barry Chin/The Boston Globe) The Department of Homeland Security featured Boston’s Fenway Park in a Tuesday night social media post, prompting prompt and biting backlash from Bostonians.

title: 2 stabbed at hummus facility in Haverhill
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News Both people were brought to local hospitals with serious injuries, Haverhill police said.
ShareSend this article to your social connections.
Two people were stabbed Tuesday morning at a hummus facility in Haverhill, police said.

title: Woman charged in crash that killed Webster 10-year-old, allegedly was driving twice the speed limit
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News Sherry Plitouke, 35, was caught on video less than a quarter-mile from the crash site going approximately 65 mph in a 30 mph speed limit zone, police wrote in court documents.
Marleigh Guevara GoFundMe ShareSend this article to your social connections.

The Southbridge woman accused of striking and killing a 10-year-old girl while driving in Webster earlier this month is now facing motor vehicle homicide charges related to the crash, prosecutors announced Tuesday.

title: 1 in 50 million: rare ‘split-lobster’ caught off Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News The chances of finding a split-lobster are one in 50 million, but crew members on a fishing boat beat those odds and caught one off of Cape Cod last week, a fishing company said.
The split-lobster in a white container.
Wellfleet Shellfish Company By Carson Lyle April 21, 2026 | 6:51 PM 1 minute to read You have a higher probability of being struck by lightning than finding a split-lobster.

title: 26-year-old identified as man killed by police in Lexington
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Local News Police responded to a home in Lexington on Saturday after a resident reported that their son had injured himself with a knife, the DA said previously.
Prosecutors identified a man shot and killed by police in Lexington over the weekend after he allegedly approached officers with a knife.
Colin Barthelemy, 26, of Lexington was shot by police on Saturday, the office of Middlesex District Attorney Marian Ryan said in a press release Tuesday evening.
